admissions_process.summary: Graduate applicants review the program requirements, submit the application file and required documents, meet program-specific admission criteria, and complete any required interview or entrance exam steps.
steps: Review the graduate program and the official catalog or graduate admissions information.
Prepare and submit the graduate application file with the required documents.
Complete any required entrance examination, interview, or additional faculty review.
Register and pay tuition according to the term instructions and payment deadlines.

required_documents.summary: HU publishes a graduate document checklist covering identity, prior academic credentials, and application materials; the catalog and admissions pages should be used together because the HTML pages are sparse.
items: Civil ID copy for Lebanese applicants
Other faculty-specific supporting documents if requested

language_requirements.summary: HU uses English as the language of instruction. Graduate English thresholds are program-specific in the catalog: MBA applicants must submit TOEFL 550 or equivalent, while MA applicants must submit TOEFL 600 or equivalent.
notes: The official catalog also references alternative standardized English tests and placement expectations.
No separate graduate language policy page with broader thresholds was identified beyond the catalog and admissions pages.

deadlines.summary: HU publishes annual application windows in the admissions section and catalog. The catalog shows Fall and Spring application deadlines for the graduate admission cycle.

tuition_model.summary: HU's official graduate catalog lists graduate tuition at USD 455 per credit hour. The tuition-fees page confirms tuition is reviewed annually and that payment/installment arrangements are tied to the term schedule.

tuition_table.faculty: Faculty of Business Administration and Economics
tuition_table.notes: The official graduate catalog lists the MBA graduate tuition at USD 455 per credit hour.

tuition_table.faculty: Faculty of Social and Behavioral Sciences
tuition_table.notes: The official graduate catalog lists the graduate tuition at USD 455 per credit hour for graduate study.

application_fee.notes: Graduate application fee / readmission fee published in the official catalog financial section.

registration_fee.notes: Registration fee for new and readmitted students published in the official catalog financial section.

reservation_fee.notes: Reservation fee published in the official catalog financial section.

installment_plan_fee.notes: Installment payment is allowed through a signed request/declaration form; the catalog describes installment-related surcharges but the rate was not normalized further in this phase.

payment_methods.summary: HU collects tuition and fees at registration and at the beginning of each term, with installment payments allowed through the university's signed request/declaration process.

payment_plans.summary: Installment payments are permitted via a signed request/declaration form. The catalog also describes installment surcharges and term-based payment timing.

scholarships.summary: HU publishes need-based financial aid and academic scholarship information; the financial aid FAQ confirms that full-time graduate students may apply, subject to limited availability.

financial_aid.summary: Graduate financial aid is available to eligible full-time graduate students on the basis of demonstrated need and academic standing.

academic_calendar.summary: HU publishes annual academic calendars and registration periods; the catalog and calendar index should be used for term planning and registration timing.

graduate_regulations.summary: Graduate Academic Information and the Graduate Handbook cover graduate rules, progress, grading, graduation, and thesis procedures.

international_students.summary: No graduate-specific international student policy was isolated in the reviewed sources; HU's international-student page is primarily undergraduate-focused, so graduate applicants should follow the general admissions and catalog guidance.

accreditation.summary: The Faculty of Business Administration and Economics is accredited by IACBE. No separate university-wide graduate accreditation statement was isolated in the reviewed sources.
